content:
3 min read
Just now

--

In enterprise environments, there are 7 common ways to host or consume a package without publishing it to the public npm registry.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

7 common ways to host or consume a package without publishing it to the public npm registry| Method | Best For | Used in Enterprise? | Example |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **1. Local Folder** | Development/testing | ✅ Yes | `npm install ../shared-lib` |
| **2. Local Tarball (.tgz)** | Offline installation, sharing builds | ✅ Yes | `npm install ./my-lib-1.0.0.tgz` |
| **3. Git Repository (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket/Azure Repos)** | Small internal libraries | ✅ Yes | `npm install github:user/lib` |
| **4. Private npm Registry** | Enterprise package management | ⭐⭐⭐ Most Common | Nexus, Verdaccio, Artifactory |
| **5. Cloud Package Registry** | Cloud-native organizations | ⭐⭐⭐ Very Common | GitHub Packages, AWS CodeArtifact, Azure Artifacts, Google Artifact Registry |
| **6. Direct URL (HTTP/HTTPS)** | Temporary distribution | Rare | `npm install https://company.com/lib.tgz` |
| **7. npm Link / Workspace / Monorepo** | Local development across projects | ⭐⭐⭐ Very Common | `npm link`, npm workspaces, Nx, Turborepo |Press enter or click to view image in full size

Host or Consume a package without publishing it to the Public NPM Registry1. Local Folder
ProjectA
ProjectBnpm install ../ProjectBor
{
"dependencies": {
"shared-lib": "file:../shared-lib"
}
}Use case

• Developing two projects simultaneously

• Local testing
2. Tarball (.tgz)
Create package
npm packProduces
my-lib-1.0.0.tgzInstall
npm install ./my-lib-1.0.0.tgzUsed when

• No internet

• Air-gapped environments

• Sharing internal builds
3. Git Repository
Can be hosted in

• GitHub

• GitLab

• Bitbucket

• Azure DevOps

• Self-hosted GitLab
Examples
npm install git+https://github.com/company/shared-lib.gitor
npm install github:company/shared-libSpecific branch
npm install github:company/shared-lib#developSpecific tag
npm install github:company/shared-lib#v2.0.1Specific commit
npm install github:company/shared-lib#4ef23ab4. Private npm Registry (Most Common)
Companies usually host internal registries such as:

• Sonatype Nexus Repository

• JFrog Artifactory

• Verdaccio

• npm Enterprise (legacy)
Example
Developer
│
▼
npm install company-ui
│
▼
Private Registry
│
▼
Company Package.npmrc
registry=https://npm.company.com/
//npm.company.com/:_authToken=xxxxxInstall
npm install @company/ui5. Cloud Package Registry
Examples

• GitHub Packages

• AWS CodeArtifact

• Azure Artifacts

• Google Artifact Registry
Example
@company:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.comInstall
npm install @company/uiCommon in cloud-based organizations.
6. Direct URL
Package hosted on a server
npm install https://company.com/packages/ui-2.0.0.tgzUseful for temporary distributions or CI/CD artifacts.
7. npm Link / Workspaces / Monorepo
npm link
cd shared-lib
npm linkcd app
npm link shared-lib
Changes are reflected immediately.
npm Workspaces
root/
package.json
packages/
ui/
api/
auth/npm installnpm links all workspace packages automatically.
Nx / Turborepo
Large organizations commonly use monorepo tools to manage multiple applications and shared libraries in a single repository with consistent dependency management.
Which methods do enterprises actually use?
| Method | Popularity |
| ------------------------- | ---------- |
| Local Folder | ⭐⭐ |
| Tarball | ⭐ |
| Git Repository | ⭐⭐⭐ |
| Private Registry | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Cloud Package Registry | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Direct URL | ⭐ |
| npm Workspaces / Monorepo | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |Real-world examples

• Google: Monorepo with internal build/package infrastructure (not public npm).

• Microsoft: Azure Artifacts and internal package feeds.

• Amazon (AWS): AWS CodeArtifact and internal repositories.

• Netflix: Internal registries and monorepo tooling.

• Banking/Financial institutions: Commonly use JFrog Artifactory, Sonatype Nexus, or Verdaccio hosted within their private networks for security and governance.
For interview purposes, it’s worth emphasizing that private registries (Artifactory, Nexus, Verdaccio, Azure Artifacts, AWS CodeArtifact) and Git-based dependencies are the most common alternatives to the public npm registry in enterprise environments.
